tools.name: create_issue
tools.description: Create a new issue in a GitHub repository with a title, body, labels, and assignees.

tools.name: create_ticket
tools.description: Create a new ticket with title and description.

tools.name: send_message
tools.description: Send a message to a Slack channel or thread. Supports rich text formatting with blocks.

tools.name: query
tools.description: Execute a read-only SQL query against a PostgreSQL database and return results as JSON rows. Supports parameterized queries to prevent SQL injection.

sql.description: SQL query to execute. Must be a SELECT statement.

params.description: Positional parameters for parameterized queries, e.g. ['value1', 42]

database.description: Database name to connect to. Defaults to the configured default.

tools.name: web_search
tools.description: Search the web using Tavily API and return structured results with titles, URLs, and content snippets. Optimized for AI agent consumption with relevance scoring.

max_results.description: Maximum number of results to return (1-10, default 5)

search_depth.description: Search depth: 'basic' for fast results, 'advanced' for comprehensive results

tools.name: find_elements
tools.description: Find elements on a web page.

tools.name: navigate
tools.description: Navigate the browser to a URL and wait for the page to load. Supports configurable wait conditions and timeout. This tool is part of the Playwright MCP server which provides full browser automation capabilities including clicking, typing, screenshots, and more. The browser runs in headless mode by default but can be configured for headed mode. Supports Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit browsers. Navigation will wait for the page to reach the 'load' state by default, but this can be configured using the waitUntil parameter. Common wait states include 'load' (default), 'domcontentloaded', and 'networkidle'. The timeout parameter controls how long to wait for the page to load before throwing an error. Default timeout is 30 seconds.
